summary: Hardcoded GitHub Personal Access Token shipped in import-time code (obfuscated as char-array)
details: whatsfly/dependencies/github_actions_download.py contains a live GitHub fine-grained Personal Access Token (`github_pat_11AZ...`) assembled at runtime from a 90+ element list of single-character strings via `''.join([...])` — a deliberate technique to evade secret scanners. The assembled token is then sent in an `Authorization: token <PAT>` header to api.github.com to download CI artifacts. This code path is reachable at import time: `whatsfly/__init__.py` calls `ensureUsableBinaries()`, which invokes `download_file()` in github_actions_download.py whenever the local Go build fails or the prebuilt binary is missing. Any installer who imports the package gains authenticated access to GitHub on behalf of the token's owner; depending on the PAT's scopes, this may include the ability to modify repository contents or CI artifacts (which in turn ship as prebuilt binaries to other installers, creating a supply-chain amplification path). The per-character obfuscation rules out simple oversight — the author actively tried to hide the credential from scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
